{"description":"LOCATION: src/content.rs:127-141, process_content()

FINDING: When a post contains a URL with a fragment like 'https://example.com/page#rust', both URL_RE and HASHTAG_RE match overlapping spans: URL_RE captures the full URL (positions 0..38), while HASHTAG_RE captures '#rust' within it (positions 34..38). The overlap-filtering loop keeps the replacement with the higher start position first — the hashtag replacement wins over the URL replacement because it has a higher start position and the URL's end (38) is not <= the hashtag's start (34).

Result: 'https://example.com/page#rust' is rendered as the bare text 'https://example.com/page' followed by a linked hashtag '#rust', instead of the entire string being rendered as a linked URL.

IMPACT: Posts containing URLs with fragment identifiers (common in documentation links, blog anchors) are incorrectly rendered. The URL prefix is not linkified and the fragment becomes a spurious hashtag with a tag object in the AP Note. This also creates a spurious hashtag entry in the ActivityPub Note's 'tag' array, potentially polluting the tag timelines of remote servers.","title":"content.rs: URL containing #fragment incorrectly linkified as hashtag instead of full URL"}
Fixed: URL#fragment no longer split into URL+hashtag; hashtags inside URLs are skipped
